title: Commending United States Border Patrol Chief Jason Owens on his retirement after 29 years of exemplary public service.
plain_summary: This bill is about honoring a man named Jason Owens who worked for the US Border Patrol for 29 years. He was the chief, which means he was the top leader of the Border Patrol. The House of Representatives wants to officially thank him and praise him for doing a great job protecting the country's borders. They are recognizing his hard work and dedication as he retires from his important job.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Homeland Security.

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution honors United States Border Patrol Chief Jason Owens on his retirement after twenty-nine years of excellent public service.

sections.title: Background Information
sections.text: The United States Border Patrol has protected Americans and helped the economy through border security since nineteen twenty-four. Jason Owens started working for the Border Patrol in nineteen ninety-six in the El Centro area as part of training class three hundred twenty-five. In two thousand one, he completed special training for the Border Patrol Tactical Unit and the Search, Trauma and Rescue Team. He then worked as a supervisor at the tactical unit headquarters in El Paso, Texas, leading missions against drug smuggling and terrorism. In two thousand seven, he was promoted to Assistant Chief at Border Patrol headquarters in Washington, DC, working with the Special Operations Division. In two thousand nine, he moved to the Grand Forks area as the agent in charge of the Pembina Station, and later became Assistant Chief Patrol Agent, managing all stations in Minnesota. In two thousand twelve, he moved to the Rio Grande Valley area and served as the Special Operations unit leader, then became the agent in charge of the Rio Grande City Station. In two thousand fifteen, he became the Division Chief of Operations for the Laredo area. In two thousand sixteen, he was assigned to Immigration and Customs Enforcement and Homeland Security Investigations as Deputy Assistant Director for Enterprise Services in Washington, DC. From two thousand seventeen to two thousand eighteen, he served as the acting chief for the Laredo area, directing operations along the United States-Mexico border and commanding over one thousand nine hundred agents and staff. In two thousand nineteen, he served as Chief Patrol Agent for the Houlton area, responsible for border security in Maine. In two thousand twenty, he served as Chief Patrol Agent at the Border Patrol Academy, providing leadership and direction for basic and advanced training courses. From two thousand twenty-one to two thousand twenty-three, he served as Chief Patrol Agent for the Del Rio area, leading one of the largest and busiest areas along the United States-Mexico border. He attended Shorter University and the National War College, studying accounting and national security strategy. He graduated from the Customs and Border Protection Leadership Institute, American University's Key Executive Leadership Program, and the Department of Homeland Security Senior Executive Service training program. Jason Owens became the twenty-sixth Chief of the United States Border Patrol on July second, two thousand twenty-three. Since the beginning of two thousand twenty-one, there has been a huge increase in the number of people illegally crossing the southern border. He strengthened efforts to fight criminal organizations by creating the Border Patrol Targeting and Investigations Division. He expanded the Border Patrol Resiliency Division to focus on the well-being of workers by providing complete physical and mental support for agents and staff. Despite the challenges this created for the brave men and women who defend our borders, Jason Owens remained committed to carrying out the Border Patrol mission and upholding the law.

sections.title: House Actions
sections.text: The House of Representatives honors United States Border Patrol Chief Jason Owens on his retirement after twenty-nine years of honorable service to the Nation. The House also recognizes and confirms that Jason Owens showed excellent leadership and faithfulness in doing his duties.
